steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must describe whatever quality made Deane "very popular" and then functioned as a tool for convincing France to help. The word has to name a type of standing or reputation — something others confer on you — not a personal character trait.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘controversy’: Being controversial is the opposite of what helps persuade a foreign government. Controversy implies division and resistance, not the goodwill needed to win an ally. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘sincerity’: Sincerity is an internal character trait — it describes how Deane acts, not how others regard him. "Very popular" signals external reputation, not inner honesty. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘esteem’: Esteem means the high regard others have for you — exactly what being "very popular" produces and exactly what gives someone the social standing to persuade a foreign power. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘thoughtfulness’: Like sincerity, thoughtfulness is a personal trait, not a measure of public reputation. It does not follow from "very popular" and does not explain persuasive leverage. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
